This case-sensitive code system https://fhir-ph-core.wah.ph/phcore/CodeSystem/PSCED defines Philippine Standard Classification of Education (PSCED) codes.

Total concepts: 1116

name: PSCEDCS
title: PSCED
description: A list of codes representing the Philippine Standard Classification of Education (PSCED).

concept.definition: PSCED Level 0 - Early Childhood Education
Broad field of EARLY CHILDHOOD EDUCATIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS at PSCED Level 0.
Narrow field: EARLY CHILDHOOD EDUCATIONAL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMS.

The educational properties of early childhood educational development are characterised by a learning environment that is visually stimulating and language-rich. These programs foster self-expression, with an emphasis on language acquisition and the use of language for meaningful communication. There are opportunities for active play, so that children can exercise their coordination and motor skills under supervision and through interaction with staff. Programs providing only childcare (supervision, nutrition and health) are not covered by PSCED.
Broad field of PRE-PRIMARY EDUCATION PROGRAMS (KINDERGARTEN) at PSCED Level 0.
Narrow field: PRE-PRIMARY EDUCATION PROGRAMS (KINDERGARTEN).

Pursuant to the Kindergarten Education Act (Republic Act No. 10157) and the Enhanced Basic Education Act of 2013 (Republic Act 10533), mandatory and compulsory Kindergarten Education that effectively promotes physical, social, cognitive, and emotional skills stimulation and values formation is offered to all five (5)-year old Filipino children to sufficiently prepare them for Grade One. The Kindergarten Education Act recognizes that Kindergarten Education is vital to the development of the child, for it is the period when the young mind's absorptive capacity is at its sharpest. It is also the policy of the State to make education responsive to the needs, circumstances, and diversity of learners, schools, and communities using developmentally-appropriate and culturally-sensitive practices.
PSCED Level 1 - Primary Education
Broad field of PRIMARY EDUCATION PROGRAMS (ELEMENTARY) at PSCED Level 1.
Narrow field: PRIMARY EDUCATION PROGRAMS (ELEMENTARY).
Detailed field: Primary Education Programs.

concept.display: Inclusive and Special Needs Education Programs
concept.definition: Special needs education is designed to facilitate learning by individuals who, for a wide variety of reasons, require additional support and adaptive pedagogical methods in order to participate and meet learning objectives in an education program. Reasons may include (but are not limited to) disadvantages in physical, behavioural, intellectual, emotional and social capacities. PSCED level 1 includes programs suited to individuals with special needs if the program provides systematic teaching and learning in the fundamental skills of reading, writing and mathematics, irrespective of the age of the participant.

PSCED Level 1 includes primary-level second chance or re-integration programs. Such education programs usually target individuals who: i) left school before completing primary education, allowing them to re-enter the education system and complete primary education; or ii) completed primary education but wish to enter an education program or occupation for which they are not yet qualified. Participants are typically older than the target age group for PSCED Level 1 (but not necessarily adults).
PSCED Level 2 - Lower Secondary Education

concept.display: Inclusive and Special Needs Education Programs
concept.definition: Special needs education is designed to facilitate learning by individuals who, for a wide variety of reasons, require additional support and adaptive pedagogical methods in order to participate and meet learning objectives in an education program. Reasons may include (but are not limited to) disadvantages in physical, behavioural, intellectual, emotional and social capacities. PSCED level 2 includes programs suited to individuals with special needs that are designed to build on the fundamental teaching and learning processes that begin at PSCED level 1 and/or to provide skills relevant to employment.

PSCED level 2 includes lower secondary-level second chance or re-integration programs. Such education programs usually target individuals who: i) left education after completing primary education but before completing lower secondary education, allowing them to re-enter the education system and complete a lower secondary education program; or ii) who completed lower secondary education but wish to enter an education program or occupation for which they are not yet qualified. Participants are typically older than the target age group for PSCED level 2.

concept.definition: Vocational education is defined as education programs that are designed for learners to acquire the knowledge, skills and competencies specific to a particular occupation, trade, or class of occupations or trades. Such programs may have work-based components (e.g. apprenticeships, dual-system education programs). Successful completion of such programs leads to labour market-relevant, vocational qualifications acknowledged as occupationally-oriented by the relevant national authorities and/or the labour market.

concept.definition: Vocational education is defined as education programs that are designed for learners to acquire the knowledge, skills and competencies specific to a particular occupation, trade, or class of occupations or trades. Such programs may have work-based components (e.g. apprenticeships, dual-system education programs). Successful completion of such programs leads to labour market-relevant, vocational qualifications acknowledged as occupationally-oriented by the relevant national authorities and/or the labour market. Learners must be required to show proper work ethics and values.
PSCED Level 4 - Post-secondary non-tertiary education
Broad field of GENERIC PROGRAMMES AND QUALIFICATIONS at PSCED Level 4.
Narrow field: Basic programmes and qualifications.

Basic programmes and qualifications are designed to provide participants with fundamental skills in reading, writing and arithmetic along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, natural science, social science, art and music, and in some cases religious instruction. These programs and qualifications are normally offered at primary and lower secondary levels. Broad, non-specialised programs at upper secondary level are also classified here even if there is some concentration on, for example, humanities, social science, natural science etc. Vocational programs and qualifications are included here only by way of exception.
Narrow field: Literacy and numeracy.

Literacy and numeracy are programs or qualifications arranged mainly for adults, designed to teach fundamental skills in reading, writing and arithmetic. The typical age range of participants can be used to distinguish between detailed field 40011 'Basic programs and qualifications' and this detailed field.
Narrow field: Personal skills and development.

Personal skills and development are defined by reference to the effects on the individual's capacity (mental, social etc.). This detailed field covers personal skills programs not included in 40011 'Basic programmes and qualifications' or 40021 'Literacy and numeracy', giving key competencies and transferable skills.

Broad field of EDUCATION at PSCED Level 4.
Narrow field: EDUCATION.
Detailed field: Education not further defined.
Education science is the study of the learning process and the theories, methods and techniques of imparting knowledge to others.

Training for pre-school teachers is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching very young children up to 7 years of age within formal school settings at pre-primary or early childhood educational development levels.

Training for teachers without subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of providing children between approximately 5 and 15 years of age with the fundamental skills in reading, writing and mathematics along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, social science etc. and laying the foundation for lifelong learning. Subject specialization is stressed less than in detailed field 40114 "Teacher training with subject specialization". The study of teaching children with special needs is included in this detailed field, likewise the study of teaching adults fundamental reading and writing skills and teaching immigrants these types of skills in their home or first language.

Teacher training with subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching a specific subject, mostly at secondary or higher levels of education. Programs and qualifications included in this detailed field often comprise both studies as well as the subject/s that are going to be taught.
Detailed field: Education not elsewhere classified.

concept.definition: Narrow field: Arts and humanities not further defined.

concept.display: Arts and humanities not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Arts and humanities not further defined.
Narrow field: Arts.
Detailed field: Arts not further defined.

Audio-visual techniques and media production is the study of techniques and skills to produce books or newspapers, radio or TV production, film or video production, recorded music production and graphic reproduction. It includes programs and qualifications in methods of color reproduction, photography and computer graphics. Study of combining pictures, words and decorations in the production of books, magazines, posters, adverts etc., is also included.
Fashion, interior and industrial design is the study of creatively combining line, form and fabric designing and constructing e.g. fashion garments, industrial products and interiors.
Fine arts is the study of visual forms of creative expression, dealing with theory, history, techniques, performance and production in fine arts.
Handicrafts is the study of techniques and skills in a chosen craft, such as jewellery, pottery, weaving, woodcarving, etc.
Music and performing arts is the study of the principles and techniques associated with performance involving music, speech, movement, mime, characterization, improvisation and stage craft.
Detailed field: Arts not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Humanities (except languages).
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not further defined.
Religion and theology is the study of religious beliefs, concepts, symbols, expressions and texts of spirituality.

History is the study of past events, especially the political, social and economic development of a country, a continent or the world. History of medicine is classified here, likewise the study of history of science and ideas. Archaeology is the study of ancient civilizations by scientific analysis of what is found in the ground.
Philosophy and ethics is the study of philosophy, ethics and related subjects dealing with the conception of life.
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Languages.
Detailed field: Languages not further defined.

Language acquisition is the study of the structure and composition of languages taught as second or foreign languages (i.e. that are intended for non-native or non-fluent speakers of the language). It includes the study of related cultures, literature, linguistics and phonetics if related to the specific language being acquired and forms part of the same program or qualification. Classical or dead languages are included here as it is assumed there are no native speakers of the language and hence the manner of teaching and the content of the curriculum are more similar to the teaching of foreign languages.

Literature and linguistics is defined as the study of language (s) intended for native, fluent or competent speakers of the language. It includes the study of related literature and linguistics and may or may not include the structure and composition of the language.
Detailed field: Languages not elsewhere classified.

Economics is the study of economic policy, economic theory and economic decision-making.
Political sciences and civics is the study of government and political principles or practice. The study of the rights and duties of the citizens are included here.
Psychology is the study of the human mind and behaviour as a result of individual differences, experience and environment.

Sociology and cultural studies is the study of human beings and the way they behave in groups and in relation to society. The study of ethnology and social anthropology are included here, likewise the study of human and social geography.

Journalism and reporting is the study of the theory and practices of journalism/reporting as part of the field of mass communication. Journalism and reporting is about the wording and content of messages. It comprises news reporting, writing commentaries and feature stories of public interest etc.

Library, information and archival studies is the study of the methods of selecting, acquiring, organizing and storing collections of information, and facilitating the use of information. Museum and library studies are included here.

concept.display: Business and administration not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Business and administration not further defined.
Accounting and taxation is the study of maintaining, auditing and recording financial transactions.

Finance, banking and insurance is the study of planning, directing, organizing and controlling financial activities and services. It includes the control and monitoring of the financial resources of organizations, institutions and individuals, and the provision of financial services at the corporate and individual level.

Management and administration is the study of planning, directing and operating the functions and activities of organizations and institutions. Programs in management also including administration, economics, finance etc. are included here if emphasis is given to management and administration.

Marketing and advertising is the study of promoting the exchange processes of goods and services between organizations and/or individuals, and the study of consumer behaviour and requirements. It includes the study of the role of product development, pricing, distribution, promotion and sales in order to maximize business performance.

Secretarial and office work is the study of administrative procedures and practices, office technology and clerical, shorthand and keyboard skills. Specialized secretarial programs (bilingual, medical, law, accounting etc.) are included if the program has secretarial work as its objective, rather than work as specialized assistants.

Wholesale and retail sales is the study of buying and selling goods and services, including stock management, pricing practices, loss prevention, sales systems and procedures. It includes the study of the workings and current trends of the wholesale and retail industries. Selling of buildings and properties is also included.

concept.display: Other work skills involving business and administration
concept.definition: Work skills is the study of the structure and function of working life. It comprises programs, mainly given as staff training, related to the working place and to work assignments.

Biochemistry sometimes called Biological chemistry, is the study of the chemistry of living organisms, including but not limited to, living matter.

Detailed field: Environment not further defined.
Environmental sciences are the study of organisms in relation to one another and to the environment.

Natural environments and wildlife is the study of the relationships between living organisms in natural environments in order to protect nature and wildlife. It includes the study of establishing and maintaining national parks in order to preserve their original natural state.
Detailed field: Environment not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Physical sciences.
Detailed field: Physical sciences not further defined.
Chemistry is the study of substances and their elements and how they react when combined.
Earth sciences is the study of the composition and structure of earth including the hydrosphere and the atmosphere.
Physics is the study of properties and interactions of matter and energy. It includes the study of astronomy and space science.
Detailed field: Physical sciences not elsewhere classified.

Mathematics is the study of abstract deductive systems. It includes algebra, arithmetic, geometry, real and complex analysis and pure and applied mathematics.
Statistics is the study of collecting, describing, arranging and analyzing numerical data. The study of probability theory and actuarial science is included.

concept.display: Engineering and engineering trades not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Engineering and engineering trades not further defined.

Chemical engineering and processes is the study of planning, designing, and developing products and processes where chemical and physical changes occur. It includes designing chemical plants and control systems.
Environmental protection technology is the study of processes in order to minimize discharge and waste and avoid pollution. It includes programs dealing with control of water, air, soil, etc.

Electricity and energy is the study of installing, maintaining, repairing and diagnosing faults in electrical wiring and related equipment in domestic, commercial and industrial establishments. Installation and maintenance of overhead and underground electrical power distribution networks is included. Energy is the study of energy generation.

Electronics and automation is the study of planning, designing, developing, maintaining and monitoring electronic equipment, machinery and systems. It includes designing computers and equipment for communication.

Mechanics and metal trades is the study of planning, designing, developing, producing, maintaining and monitoring machines, mechanical plants and systems and metal products. It includes designing and maintaining machines which produce goods and services. The focus of study in this detailed field is machines, mechanical systems and metal products.

Motor vehicles, ships and aircraft is the study of designing, developing, producing, maintaining, diagnosing faults in, repairing and servicing motor vehicles, including earth moving equipment and agriculture machines, ships, trains and aircraft. Typical is the combination of studies in both metal structures and motors.

concept.display: Engineering and engineering trades not elsewhere classified
concept.definition: Engineering studies not covered by other detailed fields are classified here: Nanotechnology.
Narrow field: Manufacturing and processing.

concept.display: Manufacturing and processing not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Manufacturing and processing not further defined.
Food processing is the study of processing and packaging of food and beverages, and the equipment and procedures used in the production and distribution of foods.

Materials is the study of the manufacturing products in glass, paper, plastic, wood or other materials like stone, clay, artificial materials, etc. Programs and qualifications in this detailed field have more to do with a specific material than general technical knowledge.
Textiles (clothes, footwear and leather) is the study of the manufacture of textiles, textile and leather products, clothing and related items, shoes and other forms of footwear.
Mining and extraction is the study of assessing, planning, developing, and directing the extraction of minerals, oil and gas from the earth.

concept.display: Manufacturing and processing not elsewhere classified
concept.definition: Detailed field: Manufacturing and processing not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Architecture and Construction.

concept.display: Architecture and construction not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Architecture and construction not further defined.

Architecture is the study of the art, science and techniques of building design. It encompasses both utilitarian ends - such as the soundness of the structure and the functional and economic efficiency of the building - and aesthetic considerations. Town planning is the study of the regulated growth and improvement of towns in both functional and aesthetical points of view.

Building is the study of the science, technology and techniques of assembling, erecting and maintaining public, commercial, industrial and residential structures and their fittings. Civil engineering is the study of planning, designing, testing and directing the construction of large scale buildings and structures, including systems for transport, water supply, sewage, etc.

Narrow field: Agriculture.
Detailed field: Agriculture not further defined.

Crop and livestock production is the study of growing, maintaining and harvesting crops and pastures, and grazing and managing animals. It includes the study of managing and maintaining farms and producing unprocessed plant and animal products.
Horticulture is the study of horticultural technology and management, floriculture, greenhouse methods, nursery management, landscape gardening, etc.
Detailed field: Agriculture not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Forestry.
Forestry is the study of establishing, cultivating, harvesting and managing forests. It includes the study of hunting and trapping.
Narrow field: Fisheries.
Fisheries is the study of breeding, rearing and harvesting fish and other seafood.
Narrow field: Veterinary.

Veterinary is the study of preventing, diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries in animals, and their general care. The study of caring for sick, injured and infirm animals undergoing treatment in veterinary clinics is included. It also includes the study of providing assistance to veterinary scientists.

Narrow field: Health.
Detailed field: Health not further defined.

Dental studies are the study of diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases and abnormalities of the teeth and gums. It includes the study of designing, making and repairing dental prostheses and orthodontic appliances. It also includes the study of providing assistance to dentists.

Medicine is the study of the principles and procedures used in preventing, diagnosing, caring for and treating illness, disease and injury in humans and the maintenance of general health. Principally, this detailed field consists of training of physicians/doctors.

Nursing is the study of providing health care for the sick, disabled or infirm and assisting physicians and other medical and health professionals diagnose and treat patients. The focus of qualifications in nursing is maintaining and caring for the health of patients during illness and rehabilitation. It includes health care of old people and health care of the disabled. Midwifery is the study of providing care to childbearing women during pregnancy, labor and birth and during the postpartum period. It also includes caring for the new born and assisting the mother with breast feeding.
Medical diagnostic and treatment technology is the study of a wide range of technologies used for diagnosing and treating diseases and handicaps.
Therapy and rehabilitation is the study of restoring normal physical conditions to those incapacitated by temporary or permanent disability.
Pharmacy is the study of drugs and their effects on humans. It includes their preparation, dispensing and administration.

concept.display: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy
concept.definition: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy is the study of alternative medicine.
Detailed field: Health not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Welfare.
Detailed field: Welfare not further defined.

concept.display: Care of elderly and of disabled adults
concept.definition: Care of elderly and of disabled adults is the study of non-medical care of the elderly and disabled.

Child care and youth services are the study of the development and non-medical care of children and youths. It includes the study of recreation and leisure activities as a social service for school children and young adults.

Social work and counselling is the study of the welfare needs of communities, specific groups and individuals and the appropriate ways of meeting these needs. The focus is on social welfare with emphasis on social policy and practice.
Detailed field: Welfare not elsewhere classified.

Travel and tourism is the study of marketing and advertising tourist destinations and events. It includes the study of ticketing and reservation practices. Leisure is the study of recreational and leisure activities for individuals and groups.

Occupational health and safety is the study of recognizing, evaluating and controlling environmental factors associated with the workplace.

PSCED Level 5 - Short-cycle tertiary education
Broad field of GENERIC PROGRAMMES AND QUALIFICATIONS at PSCED Level 5.
Narrow field: Basic programmes and qualifications.

Basic programmes and qualifications are designed to provide participants with fundamental skills in reading, writing and arithmetic along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, natural science, social science, art and music, and in some cases religious instruction. These programs and qualifications are normally offered at primary and lower secondary levels. Broad, non-specialised programs at upper secondary level are also classified here even if there is some concentration on, for example, humanities, social science, natural science etc. Vocational programs and qualifications are included here only by way of exception.
Narrow field: Literacy and numeracy.

Literacy and numeracy are programs or qualifications arranged mainly for adults, designed to teach fundamental skills in reading, writing and arithmetic. The typical age range of participants can be used to distinguish between detailed field 50011 'Basic programs and qualifications' and this detailed field.
Narrow field: Personal skills and development.

Personal skills and development are defined by reference to the effects on the individual's capacity (mental, social etc.). This detailed field covers personal skills programs not included in 50011 'Basic programmes and qualifications' or 50021 'Literacy and numeracy', giving key competencies and transferable skills.

Broad field of EDUCATION at PSCED Level 5.
Narrow field: EDUCATION.
Detailed field: Education not further defined.
Education science is the study of the learning process and the theories, methods and techniques of imparting knowledge to others.

Training for pre-school teachers is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching very young children up to 7 years of age within formal school settings at pre-primary or early childhood educational development levels.

Training for teachers without subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of providing children between approximately 5 and 15 years of age with the fundamental skills in reading, writing and mathematics along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, social science etc. and laying the foundation for lifelong learning. Subject specialization is stressed less than in detailed field 50114 "Teacher training with subject specialization". The study of teaching children with special needs is included in this detailed field, likewise the study of teaching adults fundamental reading and writing skills and teaching immigrants these types of skills in their home or first language.

Teacher training with subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching a specific subject, mostly at secondary or higher levels of education. Programs and qualifications included in this detailed field often comprise both studies as well as the subject/subjects that are going to be taught.
Detailed field: Education not elsewhere classified.

Narrow field: Arts.
Detailed field: Arts not further defined.

Audio-visual techniques and media production is the study of techniques and skills to produce books or newspapers, radio or TV production, film or video production, recorded music production and graphic reproduction. It includes programs and qualifications in methods of color reproduction, photography and computer graphics. Study of combining pictures, words and decorations in the production of books, magazines, posters, adverts etc., is also included.
Fashion, interior and industrial design is the study of creatively combining line, form and fabric designing and constructing e.g. fashion garments, industrial products and interiors.
Fine arts is the study of visual forms of creative expression, dealing with theory, history, techniques, performance and production in fine arts.
Handicrafts is the study of techniques and skills in a chosen craft, such as jewellery, pottery, weaving, woodcarving, etc.
Music and performing arts is the study of the principles and techniques associated with performance involving music, speech, movement, mime, characterization, improvisation and stage craft.
Detailed field: Arts not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Humanities (except languages).
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not further defined.
Religion and theology is the study of religious beliefs, concepts, symbols, expressions and texts of spirituality.

History is the study of past events, especially the political, social and economic development of a country, a continent or the world. History of medicine is classified here, likewise the study of history of science and ideas. Archaeology is the study of ancient civilizations by scientific analysis of what is found in the ground.
Philosophy and ethics is the study of philosophy, ethics and related subjects dealing with the conception of life.
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Languages.
Detailed field: Languages not further defined.

Language acquisition is the study of the structure and composition of languages taught as second or foreign languages (i.e. that are intended for non-native or non-fluent speakers of the language). It includes the study of related cultures, literature, linguistics and phonetics if related to the specific language being acquired and forms part of the same program or qualification. Classical or dead languages are included here as it is assumed there are no native speakers of the language and hence the manner of teaching and the content of the curriculum are more similar to the teaching of foreign languages.

Literature and linguistics is defined as the study of language (s) intended for native, fluent or competent speakers of the language. It includes the study of related literature and linguistics and may or may not include the structure and composition of the language.
Detailed field: Languages not elsewhere classified.

Accounting and taxation is the study of maintaining, auditing and recording financial transactions.

Finance, banking and insurance is the study of planning, directing, organizing and controlling financial activities and services. It includes the control and monitoring of the financial resources of organizations, institutions and individuals, and the provision of financial services at the corporate and individual level.

Management and administration is the study of planning, directing and operating the functions and activities of organizations and institutions. Programs in management also including administration, economics, finance etc. are included here if emphasis is given to management and administration.

Marketing and advertising is the study of promoting the exchange processes of goods and services between organizations and/or individuals, and the study of consumer behaviour and requirements. It includes the study of the role of product development, pricing, distribution, promotion and sales in order to maximize business performance.

Secretarial and office work is the study of administrative procedures and practices, office technology and clerical, shorthand and keyboard skills. Specialized secretarial programs (bilingual, medical, law, accounting etc.) are included if the program has secretarial work as its objective, rather than work as specialized assistants.

Wholesale and retail sales is the study of buying and selling goods and services, including stock management, pricing practices, loss prevention, sales systems and procedures. It includes the study of the workings and current trends of the wholesale and retail industries. Selling of buildings and properties is also included.
Work skills is the study of the structure and function of working life. It comprises programs, mainly given as staff training, related to the working place and to work assignments.

Chemical engineering and processes is the study of planning, designing, and developing products and processes where chemical and physical changes occur. It includes designing chemical plants and control systems.
Environmental protection technology is the study of processes in order to minimize discharge and waste and avoid pollution. It includes programs dealing with control of water, air, soil, etc.

Electricity and energy is the study of installing, maintaining, repairing and diagnosing faults in electrical wiring and related equipment in domestic, commercial and industrial establishments. Installation and maintenance of overhead and underground electrical power distribution networks is included. Energy is the study of energy generation.

Electronics and automation is the study of planning, designing, developing, maintaining and monitoring electronic equipment, machinery and systems. It includes designing computers and equipment for communication.

Mechanics and metal trades is the study of planning, designing, developing, producing, maintaining and monitoring machines, mechanical plants and systems and metal products. It includes designing and maintaining machines which produce goods and services. The focus of study in this detailed field is machines, mechanical systems and metal products.

Motor vehicles, ships and aircraft is the study of designing, developing, producing, maintaining, diagnosing faults in, repairing and servicing motor vehicles, including earth moving equipment and agriculture machines, ships, trains and aircraft. Typical is the combination of studies in both metal structures and motors.

concept.display: Engineering and engineering trades not elsewhere classified

concept.display: Manufacturing and processing not further defined

Food processing is the study of processing and packaging of food and beverages, and the equipment and procedures used in the production and distribution of foods.

Materials is the study of the manufacturing products in glass, paper, plastic, wood or other materials like stone, clay, artificial materials etc. Programs and qualifications in this detailed field have more to do with a specific material than general technical knowledge.
Textiles (clothes, footwear and leather) is the study of the manufacture of textiles, textile and leather products, clothing and related items, shoes and other forms of footwear.
Mining and extraction is the study of assessing, planning, developing, and directing the extraction of minerals, oil and gas from the earth.

It encompasses both utilitarian ends - such as the soundness of the structure and the functional and economic efficiency of the building - and aesthetic considerations. Town planning is the study of the regulated growth and improvement of towns in both functional and aesthetical points of view.

Building is the study of the science, technology and techniques of assembling, erecting and maintaining public, commercial, industrial and residential structures and their fittings. Civil engineering is the study of planning, designing, testing and directing the construction of large scale buildings and structures, including systems for transport, water supply, sewage, etc.

Crop and livestock production is the study of growing, maintaining and harvesting crops and pastures, and grazing and managing animals. It includes the study of managing and maintaining farms and producing unprocessed plant and animal products.
Horticulture is the study of horticultural technology and management, floriculture, greenhouse methods, nursery management, landscape gardening, etc.
Detailed field: Agriculture not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Forestry.
Forestry is the study of establishing, cultivating, harvesting and managing forests. It includes the study of hunting and trapping.
Narrow field: Fisheries.
Fisheries is the study of breeding, rearing and harvesting fish and other seafood.
Narrow field: Veterinary.

Veterinary is the study of preventing, diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries in animals, and their general care. The study of caring for sick, injured and infirm animals undergoing treatment in veterinary clinics is included. It also includes the study of providing assistance to veterinary scientists.

Dental studies are the study of diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases and abnormalities of the teeth and gums. It includes the study of designing, making and repairing dental prostheses and orthodontic appliances. It also includes the study of providing assistance to dentists.

Medicine is the study of the principles and procedures used in preventing, diagnosing, caring for and treating illness, disease and injury in humans and the maintenance of general health. Principally, this detailed field consists of training of physicians/doctors.

Nursing is the study of providing health care for the sick, disabled or infirm and assisting physicians and other medical and health professionals diagnose and treat patients. The focus of qualifications in nursing is maintaining and caring for the health of patients during illness and rehabilitation. It includes health care of old people and health care of the disabled. Midwifery is the study of providing care to childbearing women during pregnancy, labor and birth and during the postpartum period. It also includes caring for the new born and assisting the mother with breast feeding.
Medical diagnostic and treatment technology is the study of a wide range of technologies used for diagnosing and treating diseases and handicaps.
Therapy and rehabilitation is the study of restoring normal physical conditions to those incapacitated by temporary or permanent disability.
Pharmacy is the study of drugs and their effects on humans. It includes their preparation, dispensing and administration.

Social work and counselling is the study of the welfare needs of communities, specific groups and individuals and the appropriate ways of meeting these needs. The focus is on social welfare with emphasis on social policy and practice.

Education science is the study of the learning process and the theories, methods and techniques of imparting knowledge to others.

Training for pre-school teachers is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching very young children up to 7 years of age within formal school settings at pre-primary or early childhood educational development levels.

Training for teachers without subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of providing children between approximately 5 and 15 years of age with the fundamental skills in reading, writing and mathematics along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, social science etc. and laying the foundation for lifelong learning. Subject specialization is stressed less than in detailed field 60114 "Teacher training with subject specialization". The study of teaching children with special needs is included in this detailed field, likewise the study of teaching adults fundamental reading and writing skills and teaching immigrants these types of skills in their home or first language.

Teacher training with subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching a specific subject, mostly at secondary or higher levels of education. Programs and qualifications included in this detailed field often comprise both studies as well as the subject/subjects that are going to be taught.
Detailed field: Education not elsewhere classified.

Audio-visual techniques and media production is the study of techniques and skills to produce books or newspapers, radio or TV production, film or video production, recorded music production and graphic reproduction. It includes programs and qualifications in methods of color reproduction, photography and computer graphics. Study of combining pictures, words and decorations in the production of books, magazines, posters, adverts etc, is also included.
Fashion, interior and industrial design is the study of creatively combining line, form and fabric designing and constructing e.g. fashion garments, industrial products and interiors.
Fine arts is the study of visual forms of creative expression, dealing with theory, history, techniques, performance and production in fine arts.
Handicrafts is the study of techniques and skills in a chosen craft, such as jewellery, pottery, weaving, woodcarving, etc.
Music and performing arts is the study of the principles and techniques associated with performance involving music, speech, movement, mime, characterization, improvization and stage craft.
Detailed field: Arts not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Humanities (except languages).
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not further defined.
Religion and theology is the study of religious beliefs, concepts, symbols, expressions and texts of spirituality.

History is the study of past events, especially the political, social and economic development of a country, a continent or the world. History of medicine is classified here, likewise the study of history of science and ideas. Archaeology is the study of ancient civilizations by scientific analysis of what is found in the ground.
Philosophy and ethics is the study of philosophy, ethics and related subjects dealing with the conception of life.
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Languages.
Detailed field: Languages not further defined.

Language acquisition is the study of the structure and composition of languages taught as second or foreign languages (i.e. that are intended for non-native or non-fluent speakers of the language). It includes the study of related cultures, literature, linguistics and phonetics if related to the specific language being acquired and forms part of the same program or qualification. Classical or dead languages are included here as it is assumed there are no native speakers of the language and hence the manner of teaching and the content of the curriculum are more similar to the teaching of foreign languages.

Literature and linguistics is defined as the study of language (s) intended for native, fluent or competent speakers of the language. It includes the study of related literature and linguistics and may or may not include the structure and composition of the language.
Detailed field: Languages not elsewhere classified.

Accounting and taxation is the study of maintaining, auditing and recording financial transactions.

Finance, banking and insurance is the study of planning, directing, organizing and controlling financial activities and services. It includes the control and monitoring of the financial resources of organizations, institutions and individuals, and the provision of financial services at the corporate and individual level.

Management and administration is the study of planning, directing and operating the functions and activities of organizations and institutions. Programs in management also including administration, economics, finance, etc. are included here if emphasis is given to management and administration.

Marketing and advertising is the study of promoting the exchange processes of goods and services between organizations and/or individuals, and the study of consumer behavior and requirements. It includes the study of the role of product development, pricing, distribution, promotion and sales in order to maximize business performance.

Secretarial and office work is the study of administrative procedures and practices, office technology and clerical, shorthand and keyboard skills. Specialized secretarial programs (bilingual, medical, law, accounting etc.) are included if the program has secretarial work as its objective, rather than work as specialized assistants.

Wholesale and retail sales is the study of buying and selling goods and services, including stock management, pricing practices, loss prevention, sales systems and procedures. It includes the study of the workings and current trends of the wholesale and retail industries. Selling of buildings and properties is also included.
Work skills is the study of the structure and function of working life. It comprises programs, mainly given as staff training, related to the working place and to work assignments.

Chemical engineering and processes is the study of planning, designing, and developing products and processes where chemical and physical changes occur. It includes designing chemical plants and control systems.
Environmental protection technology is the study of processes in order to minimize discharge and waste and avoid pollution.

Electricity and energy is the study of installing, maintaining, repairing and diagnosing faults in electrical wiring and related equipment in domestic, commercial and industrial establishments. Installation and maintenance of overhead and underground electrical power distribution networks is included. Energy is the study of energy generation.

Electronics and automation is the study of planning, designing, developing, maintaining and monitoring electronic equipment, machinery and systems. It includes designing computers and equipment for communication.

Mechanics and metal trades is the study of planning, designing, developing, producing, maintaining and monitoring machines, mechanical plants and systems and metal products. It includes designing and maintaining machines which produce goods and services. The focus of study in this detailed field is machines, mechanical systems and metal products.

Motor vehicles, ships and aircraft is the study of designing, developing, producing, maintaining, diagnosing faults in, repairing and servicing motor vehicles, including earth moving equipment and agriculture machines, ships, trains and aircraft. Typical is the combination of studies in both metal structures and motors.

Food processing is the study of processing and packaging of food and beverages, and the equipment and procedures used in the production and distribution of foods.

Programs and qualifications with the following main content are classified here: Boat building (non-motor); Cabinet making; Carpentry (furniture); Ceramics (industrial); Furniture making; Glass working (industrial); Industrial diamond production; Paper manufacturing and processing; Plastic manufacturing; Rubber processing; Timber technology; Wood machining and turning; Woodwork trades.
Textiles (clothes, footwear and leather) is the study of the manufacture of textiles, textile and leather products, clothing and related items, shoes and other forms of footwear.
Mining and extraction is the study of assessing, planning, developing, and directing the extraction of minerals, oil and gas from the earth.

It encompasses both utilitarian ends - such as the soundness of the structure and the functional and economic efficiency of the building - and aesthetic considerations. Town planning is the study of the regulated growth and improvement of towns in both functional and aesthetical points of view.

Building is the study of the science, technology and techniques of assembling, erecting and maintaining public, commercial, industrial and residential structures and their fittings. Civil engineering is the study of planning, designing, testing and directing the construction of large scale buildings and structures, including systems for transport, water supply, sewage, etc.

Horticulture is the study of horticultural technology and management, floriculture, greenhouse methods, nursery management, landscape gardening, etc.
Detailed field: Agriculture not elsewhere classified.

Forestry is the study of establishing, cultivating, harvesting and managing forests. It includes the study of hunting and trapping.

Fisheries is the study of breeding, rearing and harvesting fish and other seafood.
Narrow field: Veterinary.

Veterinary is the study of preventing, diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries in animals, and their general care. The study of caring for sick, injured and infirm animals undergoing treatment in veterinary clinics is included. It also includes the study of providing assistance to veterinary scientists.

Dental studies are the study of diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases and abnormalities of the teeth and gums. It includes the study of designing, making and repairing dental prostheses and orthodontic appliances. It also includes the study of providing assistance to dentists.

Medicine is the study of the principles and procedures used in preventing, diagnosing, caring for and treating illness, disease and injury in humans and the maintenance of general health. Principally, this detailed field consists of training of physicians/doctors.

Nursing is the study of providing health care for the sick, disabled or infirm and assisting physicians and other medical and health professionals diagnose and treat patients. The focus of qualifications in nursing is maintaining and caring for the health of patients during illness and rehabilitation. It includes health care of old people and health care of the disabled. Midwifery is the study of providing care to childbearing women during pregnancy, labor and birth and during the postpartum period. It also includes caring for the new born and assisting the mother with breast feeding.
Medical diagnostic and treatment technology is the study of a wide range of technologies used for diagnosing and treating diseases and handicaps.
Therapy and rehabilitation is the study of restoring normal physical conditions to those incapacitated by temporary or permanent disability.
Pharmacy is the study of drugs and their effects on humans. It includes their preparation, dispensing and administration.

Child care and youth services are the study of the development and non-medical care of children and youths. It includes the study of recreation and leisure activities as a social service for school children and young adults.

Social work and counselling is the study of the welfare needs of communities, specific groups and individuals and the appropriate ways of meeting these needs. The focus is on social welfare with emphasis on social policy and practice.

Wholesale and retail sales is the study of buying and selling goods and services, including stock management, pricing practices, loss prevention, sales systems and procedures. It includes the study of the workings and current trends of the wholesale and retail industries. Selling of buildings and properties is also included.
Work skills is the study of the structure and fucntion of working life. It comprises programs, mainly given as staff training, related to the working place and to work assignments.

Narrow field: Engineering and engineering trades.

Chemical engineering and processes is the study of planning, designing, and developing products and processes where chemical and physical changes occur. It includes designing chemical plants and control systems.
Environmental protection technology is the study of processes in order to minimize discharge and waste and avoid pollution. It includes programs dealing with control of water, air, soil, etc.

Electricity and energy is the study of installing, maintaining, repairing and diagnosing faults in electrical wiring and related equipment in domestic, commercial and industrial establishments. Installation and maintenance of overhead and underground electrical power distribution networks is included. Energy is the study of energy generation.Programs and qualifications with the following main content are classified here: Air-conditioning trades; Climate engineering; Electrical appliances repairing; Electrical engineering; Electrical fitting; Electrical power generation; Electrical trades; Energy studies; Gas distribution; Heating trades; Nuclear, hydraulic and thermal energy; Power line installation and maintenance; Power production; Refrigeration; Solar power; Wind turbines. The study of installing, diagnosing faults in and repairing heating, air-conditioning and refrigeration equipment is included here.

Electronics and automation is the study of planning, designing, developing, maintaining and monitoring electronic equipment, machinery and systems. It includes designing computers and equipment for communication.

Mechanics and metal trades is the study of planning, designing, developing, producing, maintaining and monitoring machines, mechanical plants and systems and metal products. It includes designing and maintaining machines which produce goods and services. The focus of study in this detailed field is machines, mechanical systems and metal products.

Motor vehicles, ships and aircraft is the study of designing, developing, producing, maintaining, diagnosing faults in, repairing and servicing motor vehicles, including earth moving equipment and agriculture machines, ships, trains and aircraft. Typical is the combination of studies in both metal structures and motors.

concept.display: Engineering and engineering trades not esewhere classified

Food processing is the study of processing and packaging of food and beverages, and the equipment and procedures used in the production and distribution of foods.

Materials is the study of the manufacturing products in glass, paper, plastic, wood or other materials like stone, clay, artificial materials etc. Programs and qualifications in this detailed field have more to do with a specific material than general technical knowledge.
Textiles (clothes, footwear and leather) is the study of the manufacture of textiles, textile and leather products, clothing and related items, shoes and other forms of footwear.
Mining and extraction is the study of assessing, planning, developing, and directing the extraction of minerals, oil and gas from the earth.

It encompasses both utilitarian ends - such as the soundness of the structure and the functional and economic efficiency of the building - and aesthetic considerations. Town planning is the study of the regulated growth and improvement of towns in both functional and aesthetical points of view.

Building is the study of the science, technology and techniques of assembling, erecting and maintaining public, commercial, industrial and residential structures and their fittings. Civil engineering is the study of planning, designing, testing and directing the construction of large scale buildings and structures, including systems for transport, water supply, sewage, etc.

Horticulture is the study of horticultural technology and management, floriculture, greenhouse methods, nursery management, landscape gardening, etc.

Forestry is the study of establishing, cultivating, harvesting and managing forests. It includes the study of hunting and trapping.

Fisheries is the study of breeding, rearing and harvesting fish and other seafood.
Narrow field: Veterinary.

Veterinary is the study of preventing, diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries in animals, and their general care. The study of caring for sick, injured and infirm animals undergoing treatment in veterinary clinics is included. It also includes the study of providing assistance to veterinary scientists.

Narrow field: Health.

Dental studies are the study of diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases and abnormalities of the teeth and gums. It includes the study of designing, making and repairing dental prostheses and orthodontic appliances. It also includes the study of providing assistance to dentists.

Medicine is the study of the principles and procedures used in preventing, diagnosing, caring for and treating illness, disease and injury in humans and the maintenance of general health. Principally, this detailed field consists of training of physicians/doctors.

Nursing is the study of providing health care for the sick, disabled or infirm and assisting physicians and other medical and health professionals diagnose and treat patients. The focus of qualifications in nursing is maintaining and caring for the health of patients during illness and rehabilitation. It includes health care of old people and health care of the disabled. Midwifery is the study of providing care to childbearing women during pregnancy, labor and birth and during the postpartum period. It also includes caring for the new born and assisting the mother with breast feeding.
Medical diagnostic and treatment technology is the study of a wide range of technologies used for diagnosing and treating diseases and handicaps.
Therapy and rehabilitation is the study of restoring normal physical conditions to those incapacitated by temporary or permanent disability.
Pharmacy is the study of drugs and their effects on humans. It includes their preparation, dispensing and administration.

concept.display: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy
concept.definition: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy is the study of alternative medicine.
Detailed field: Health not elsewhere classified.

concept.definition: Care of elderly and of disabled adults is the study of non-medical care of the elderly and disabled.

Child care and youth services are the study of the development and non-medical care of children and youths. It includes the study of recreation and leisure activities as a social service for school children and young adults.

Social work and counselling is the study of the welfare needs of communities, specific groups and individuals and the appropriate ways of meeting these needs. The focus is on social welfare with emphasis on social policy and practice.

Hotel, restaurants and catering is the study of providing food, beverages, accommodation and related services at hotels, restaurants, etc.

Occupational health and safety is the study of recognizing, evaluating and controlling environmental factors associated with the workplace.

Education science is the study of the learning process and the theories, methods and techniques of imparting knowledge to others.

Training for pre-school teachers is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching very young children up to 7 years of age within formal school settings at pre-primary or early childhood educational development levels.

Training for teachers without subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of providing children between approximately 5 and 15 years of age with the fundamental skills in reading, writing and mathematics along with an elementary understanding of other subjects such as history, geography, social science etc. and laying the foundation for lifelong learning. Subject specialization is stressed less than in deatiled field 80114 "Teacher training with subject specialization". The study of teaching children with special needs is included in this detailed field, likewise the study of teaching adults fundamental reading and writing skills and teaching immigrants these types of skills in their home or first language.

Teacher training with subject specialization is the study of the theories, methods and practice of teaching a specific subject, mostly at secondary or higher levels of education. Programs and qualifications included in this detailed field often comprise both studies as well as the subject/s that are going to be taught.

Audio-visual techniques and media production is the study of techniques and skills to produce books or newspapers, radio or TV production, film or video production, recorded music production and graphic reproduction. It includes programs and qualifications in methods of color reproduction, photography and computer graphics. Study of combining pictures, words and decorations in the production of books, magazines, posters, adverts, etc. is also included.
Fashion, interior and industrial design is the study of creatively combining line, form and fabric designing and constructing e.g. fashion garments, industrial products and interiors.
Fine arts is the study of viusal forms of creative expression, dealing with theory, history, techniques, performance and production in fine arts.
Handicrafts is the study of techniques and skills in a chosen craft, such as jewellery, pottery, weaving, woodcarving, etc.
Music and performing arts is the study of the principles and techniques associated with performance involving music, speech, movement, mime, characterization, improvization and stage craft.
Detailed field: Arts not elsewhere classified.

Religion and theology is the study of religious beliefs, concepts, symbols, expressions and texts of spirituality.

History is the study of past events, especially the political, social and economic development of a country, a continent or the world. History of medicine is classified here, likewise the study of history of science and ideas. Archaeology is the study of ancient civilizations by scientific analysis of what is found in the ground.
Philosophy and ethics is the study of philosophy, ethics and related subjects dealing with the conception of life.
Detailed field: Humanities (except languages) not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Languages.
Detailed field: Languages not further defined.

Language acquisition is the study of the structure and composition of languages taught as second or foreign languages (i.e. that are intended for non-native or non-fluent speakers of the language). It includes the study of related cultures, literature, linguistics and phonetics if related to the specific language being acquired and forms part of the same program or qualification. Classical or dead languages are included here as it is assumed there are no native speakers of the language and hence the manner of teaching and the content of the curriculum are more similar to the teaching of foreign languages.

Literature and linguistics is defined as the study of language(s) intended for native, fluent or competent speakers of the language. It includes the study of related literature and linguistics and may or may not include the structure and composition of the language.

Statistics is the study of collecting, describing, arranging and analyzing numerical data. The study of probability theory and actuarial science is included.

Broad field of ENGINEERING, MANUFACTURING AND CONSTRUCTION at PSCED Level 8.
Narrow field: Engineering and engineering trades.

Chemical engineering and processes is the study of planning, designing, and developing products and processes where chemical and physical changes occur. It includes designing chemical plants and control systems.
Environmental protection technology is the study of processes in order to minimize discharge and waste and avoid pollution. It includes programs dealing with control of water, air, soil, etc.

Electricity and energy is the study of installing, maintaining, repairing and diagnosing faults in electrical wiring and related equipment in domestic, commercial and industrial establishments. Installation and maintenance of overhead and underground electrical power distribution networks is included. Energy is the study of energy generation.

Electronics and automation is the study of planning, designing, developing, maintaining and monitoring electronic equipment, machinery and systems. It includes designing computers and equipment for communication.

Mechanics and metal trades is the study of planning, designing, developing, producing, maintaining and monitoring machines, mechanical plants and systems and metal products. It includes designing and maintaining machines which produce goods and services. The focus of study in this detailed field is machines, mechanical systems and metal products.

Motor vehicles, ships and aircraft is the study of designing, developing, producing, maintaining, diagnosing faults in, repairing and servicing motor vehicles, including earth moving equipment and agriculture machines, ships, trains and aircraft. Typical is the combination of studies in both metal structures and motors.

concept.display: Engineering and engineering trades not esewhere classified
concept.definition: Engineering studies not covered by other detailed fields are classified here: Nanotechnology.
Narrow field: Manufacturing and processing.

concept.display: Manufacturing and processing not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Manufacturing and processing not further defined.

Programs and qualifications with the following main content are classified here: Baking; Beer brewing; Butchery; Confectionery; Dairy foods; Food and drink processing; Food preservation; Food science and technology; Meat processing; Pastry cooking; Tobacco processing; Wine production. Study of food handling and food hygiene is included here.

Materials is the study of the manufacturing products in glass, paper, plastic, wood or other materials like stone, clay, artificial materials etc. Programs and qualifications in this detailed field have more to do with a specific material than general technical knowledge.
Textiles (clothes, footwear and leather) is the study of the manufacture of textiles, textile and leather products, clothing and related items, shoes and other forms of footwear.
Mining and extraction is the study of assessing, planning, developing, and directing the extraction of minerals, oil and gas from the earth.

concept.display: Manufacturing and processing not elsewhere classified
concept.definition: Detailed field: Manufacturing and processing not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Architecture and construction.

concept.display: Architecture and construction not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Architecture and construction not further defined.

Architecture is the study of the art, science and techniques of building design. It encompasses both utilitarian ends - such as the soundness of the structure and the functional and economic efficiency of the building - and aesthetic considerations. Town planning is the study of the regulated growth and improvement of towns in both functional and aesthetical points of view.

Building is the study of the science, technology and techniques of assembling, erecting and maintaining public, commercial, industrial and residential structures and their fittings. Civil engineering is the study of planning, designing, testing and directing the construction of large scale buildings and structures, including systems for transport, water supply, sewage, etc.

Broad field of AGRICULTURE, FORESTRY, FISHERIES AND VETERINARY at PSCED Level 8.

Narrow field: Agriculture.

Crop and livestock production is the study of growing, maintaining and harvesting crops and pastures, and grazing and managing animals. It includes the study of managing and maintaining farms and producing unprocessed plant and animal products.
Horticulture is the study of horticultural technology and management, floriculture, greenhouse methods, nursery management, landscape gardening, etc.
Detailed field: Agriculture not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Forestry.
Forestry is the study of establishing, cultivating, harvesting and managing forests. It includes the study of hunting and trapping.
Narrow field: Fisheries.
Fisheries is the study of breeding, rearing and harvesting fish and other seafood.
Narrow field: Veterinary.

Veterinary is the study of preventing, diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries in animals, and their general care. The study of caring for sick, injured and infirm animals undergoing treatment in veterinary clinics is included. It also includes the study of providing assistance to veterinary scientists.

Broad field of HEALTH AND WELFARE at PSCED Level 8.

concept.display: Health and welfare not further defined
concept.definition: Narrow field: Health and welfare not further defined.

concept.display: Health and welfare not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Health and welfare not further defined.
Narrow field: Health.

Dental studies is the study of diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases and abnormalities of the teeth and gums. It includes the study of designing, making and repairing dental prostheses and orthodontic appliances. It also includes the study of providing assistance to dentists.

Medicine is the study of the principles and procedures used in preventing, diagnosing, caring for and treating illness, disease and injury in humans and the maintenance of general health. Principally, this detailed field consists of training of physicians/doctors.

Nursing is the study of providing health care for the sick, disabled or infirm and assisting physicians and other medical and health professionals diagnose and treat patients. The focus of qualifications in nursing is maintaining and caring for the health of patients during illness and rehabilitation. It includes health care of old people and health care of the disabled. Midwifery is the study of providing care to childbearing women during pregnancy, labor and birth and during the postpartum period. It also includes caring for the new born and assisting the mother with breast feeding.
Medical diagnostic and treatment technology is the study of a wide range of technologies used for diagnosing and treating diseases and handicaps.
Therapy and rehabilitation is the study of restoring normal physical conditions to those incapacitated by temporary or permanent disability.
Pharmacy is the study of drugs and their effects on humans. It includes their preparation, dispensing and administration.

concept.display: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy
concept.definition: Traditional and complementary medicine and therapy is the study of alternative medicine.
Detailed field: Health not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Welfare.
Detailed field: Welfare not further defined.

concept.display: Care of elderly and of disabled adults
concept.definition: Care of elderly and of disabled adults is the study of non-medical care of the elderly and disabled.

Child care and youth services are the study of the development and non-medical care of children and youths. It includes the study of recreation and leisure activities as a social service for school children and young adults.

Social work and counselling is the study of the welfare needs of communities, specific groups and individuals and the appropriate ways of meeting these needs. The focus is on social welfare with emphasis on social policy and practice.

Narrow field: Services not further defined.
Detailed field: Services not further defined.
Narrow field: Personal services.
Domestic services is the study of various domestic services, such as housekeeping, cleaning, laundering, sewing, etc.
Hair and beauty services are the study of caring for the hair and the body for beautification.
Hotel, restaurants and catering is the study of providing food, beverages, accommodation and related services at hotels, restaurants, etc.
Sports is the study of techniques and skills in a chosen sport.

Travel and tourism is the study of marketing and advertising tourist destinations and events. It includes the study of ticketing and reservation practices. Leisure is the study of recreational and leisure activities for individuals and groups.
Detailed field: Personal services not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Hygiene and occupational health services.

concept.display: Hygiene and occupational health services not further defined
concept.definition: Detailed field: Hygiene and occupational health services not further defined.

Community sanitation is the study of community services dealing with items that affect public health such as hygienic standards in food, water supply, disposal of sewage and garbage and street cleaning.
Occupational health and safety is the study of recognizing, evaluating and controlling environmental factors associated with the workplace.

concept.display: Hygiene and occupational health services not elsewhere classified
concept.definition: Detailed field: Hygiene and occupational health services not elsewhere classified.
Narrow field: Security services.
Detailed field: Security services not further defined.
Military and defense is the study of services to the community connected with war and defense, designed to provide training in the principles and practice of military science.

Protection of persons and property is the study of services to the community concerning protection of property and persons. It comprises training in police work, public security, fire protection and fire-fighting.
Narrow field: Transport services.
Transport services is the study of operating, navigating and directing ships, train, aircraft and other forms of transportation.
